q: U Mya earns very little. He cannot keep his family in comfort. (Use 'too ...... to'.) 2024
a: U Mya earns too little to keep his family in comfort.

q: The box was very heavy. I could not lift it. (Join the sentences, using 'too ... to'.) 2024
a: The box was too heavy for me to lift.

q: She is very young. She cannot drive a car. (Join the sentences, using 'too ... to' / 'enough ... to'.) 2025
a: She is too young to drive a car.

q: The teacher didn't speak loudly enough for the students at the back to hear clearly. (Rewrite the sentence, using 'too ... to'.) 2026
a: The teacher spoke too softly/quietly for the students at the back to hear clearly.
